senses.definition: a country's rules that all of its citizens are required to obey

examples.x: Breaking the law can result in serious consequences.
They studied constitutional law in law school.
It's important to know your rights under the law.

senses.definition: to officially state that something, such as a principle or rule must be obeyed

examples.x: The constitution lays down the basic principles of government.
The company laid down new rules for employee conduct.
The teacher laid down the rules for the classroom.

senses.definition: approved or allowed by the law
the quality of being more compassionate, merciful, or permissive than expected, especially in terms of punishment in a court case
(of a person)  tolerant, flexible, or relaxed in enforcing rules or standards, often forgiving and understanding toward others

examples.x: The lenient teacher allowed students extra time to complete assignments if they had valid reasons for delays.
The lenient parent often overlooked minor misbehavior and preferred to guide their child with patience rather than harsh punishment.
Despite the seriousness of the offense, the judge opted for a lenient sentence, considering the defendant's remorse and efforts towards rehabilitation.
The lenient supervisor granted flexible work hours to employees who needed to balance personal responsibilities with their job duties.
In contrast to his strict predecessor, the new manager took a lenient approach to employee tardiness, focusing more on productivity than punctuality.

senses.definition: in a manner that is less strict when punishing someone or when enforcing a law

examples.x: The judge decided to sentence the first-time offender leniently, considering their remorse.
He manages his team leniently, preferring to motivate rather than reprimand.
The company policy is applied leniently for minor infractions, focusing more on improvement than punishment.
The coach treated the player's mistake leniently, understanding it was unintentional.
The judge sentenced the first-time offender leniently, taking into account their remorse and cooperation.

senses.definition: to treat a specific case differently from the usual rule or practice

examples.x: The teacher decided to make an exception and allow the student to submit the assignment a day late due to unforeseen circumstances.
The manager decided to make an exception and allow late submissions for the assignment.
Although the policy is strict, they made an exception for medical emergencies.
We don't usually accept returns without a receipt, but I can make an exception this time.
The company policy prohibits remote work, but they made an exception for employees with special needs.

senses.definition: used to show that something is very important and needs to happen

examples.x: Students must submit their applications before the deadline.
Employees must attend the mandatory training session next week.
Drivers must obey traffic laws to ensure road safety.
Guests must follow the established dress code for the formal event.
Participants must complete the survey to provide valuable feedback.

senses.definition: unable to be changed or avoided

examples.x: The rise of technology is a necessary consequence of human progress.
Change is a necessary part of life.
Death is a necessary result of living.
Their success was the necessary outcome of their hard work and preparation.
The flooding was a necessary consequence of the heavy rain and poor drainage system.

senses.definition: the fact that something must happen or is needed

examples.x: In many jobs, having a reliable internet connection is a necessity for performing daily tasks effectively.
The necessity of following traffic laws ensures safety on the roads for everyone.
For successful project completion, meeting deadlines is a necessity to keep the workflow on track.
Learning to communicate effectively is a necessity for building strong relationships in both personal and professional settings.
The doctor explained the necessity of taking medication regularly.

senses.definition: a condition or situation in which something is necessary

examples.x: He feels a deep need to be successful.
Her need for attention often caused problems.
My main need right now is a good night's sleep.
The refugees have urgent needs for food and shelter.
The school was set up in response to a local need.

senses.definition: refusal to behave as expected or failure to obey or follow rules, commands, etc.
refusing to follow a law or rule

examples.x: The company was fined for being non-compliant with environmental regulations.
Non-compliant behavior can lead to disciplinary action in the workplace.
The patient's non-compliant attitude with medication instructions resulted in health complications.
Non-compliant vehicles were ticketed during the roadside inspection.
The landlord issued a notice to the tenant for being non-compliant with the lease agreement.

senses.definition: failure to comply with a rule, obligation, etc.
the state of being forced to do something in a way that conforms to the law or is morally acceptable
necessary as a result of a rule or law

examples.x: The completion of safety training is obligatory for all new employees before they start work.
It is obligatory for all citizens to pay their taxes by the specified deadline.
Attending the weekly team meetings is obligatory for all members.
In some cultures, it is obligatory to remove your shoes before entering someone's home.
Filling out the necessary paperwork is obligatory before starting a new job.

senses.definition: to comply with laws or regulations

examples.x: The company ensures that all employees observe workplace safety regulations.
It is important for businesses to observe tax laws and regulations to avoid penalties.
As a responsible citizen, it is crucial to observe traffic laws and regulations to promote road safety.
The school strictly observes anti-bullying policies to create a safe and inclusive learning environment for students.
The restaurant must observe food safety regulations to maintain hygiene standards and prevent foodborne illnesses.

senses.definition: used when an agreement can only be reached under a specific condition
to give an instruction to someone to do something through one's authority

examples.x: The general ordered the troops to advance to the front lines.
The manager ordered the team to complete the project by the end of the week.
The teacher ordered the students to remain quiet during the exam.
The supervisor ordered the employees to attend the mandatory training session.
The captain ordered the crew to prepare for an emergency landing.

senses.definition: an official rule or order that is imposed by the law or someone with authority

examples.x: Residents were asked to comply with the water conservation ordinance during the drought.
The city passed an ordinance requiring all homes to have smoke detectors.
The city council passed an ordinance banning smoking in public parks.
Local ordinances regulate matters such as zoning, noise levels, and pet ownership within a municipality.
Violating an ordinance can result in fines or other penalties imposed by the local government.

senses.definition: to do things as one sees fit, not according to laws or rules

examples.x: Despite the company guidelines, she preferred to play by her own rules when managing her team.
As an independent artist, he decided to play by his own rules, ignoring the traditional pathways to success.
The innovative entrepreneur built his startup by playing by his own rules, defying industry norms.
In the world of high-stakes poker, he was known for playing by his own rules, often surprising his opponents.
She found success in the fashion industry by playing by her own rules and setting unique trends.

senses.definition: to oversee and enforce laws, regulations, or safety measures in a specific area, typically carried out by law enforcement or responsible authorities

examples.x: The officers diligently police the neighborhood to ensure community safety.
Law enforcement agencies are responsible for policing their jurisdiction.
The security team works to police the premises and prevent unauthorized access.
The park ranger's duty is to police the natural reserve and protect its wildlife.
Authorities must police online platforms to prevent illegal activities and ensure user safety.

senses.definition: the control and regulation of law and order by the police force or other official groups
a condition that must be met or established before other things can occur or be considered
something that is required as a precondition for something else following

examples.x: A bachelor's degree is a prerequisite for admission to the master's program.
Proof of vaccination is a prerequisite for school enrollment.
Basic computer skills are a prerequisite for the job application.
An internship is a prerequisite for graduating from the business school.
Completing the introductory course is a prerequisite for enrolling in advanced classes.

senses.definition: necessary or indispensable as a prior condition before something else can happen
(of a law or a rule) to specify that it is obligatory for something to be done

examples.x: The company policy provides that all employees must undergo annual safety training.
The contract provides that the buyer must make full payment within 30 days of receiving the invoice.
The school regulations provide that students must wear the approved uniform during school hours.
The building code provides that fire sprinkler systems are mandatory in all commercial buildings.
The local ordinance provides that pet owners must clean up after their animals in public spaces.

senses.definition: used for stating conditions necessary for something to happen or be available

examples.x: The event will proceed outdoors, provided the weather cooperates.
He was allowed to leave early, provided he finished his work by the end of the day.
We will support the proposal, provided there are no major objections from the committee.

senses.definition: on the condition that; understanding that
a condition that needs accepting before making an agreement

examples.x: She agreed to sell the property with the proviso that she can stay in the house until the end of the month.
The merger will proceed, but there's a proviso that all current employees retain their positions for at least a year.
